post.summary:
LoginWithKubernetes authenticates a workload via its projected
ServiceAccount JWT and returns a scoped AuthToken bound to a
role-policy mapping in the backend.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
LoginWithKubernetesRequest is the request message for
LoginWithKubernetes. The caller presents its projected
ServiceAccount JWT; the implementation verifies it against
Kubernetes' TokenReview API (or equivalent) and returns a scoped
AuthToken.

post.summary:
RenewLease extends the expiry of a previously issued AuthToken
or other lease. Implementations cap the renewal window at their
configured maximum.

parameters.name: leaseId
parameters.description: Identifier of the lease to renew.

parameters.name: leaseId
parameters.description: Identifier of the lease to revoke.

get.summary:
GetSecret reads a KV secret by path. Optional `version` selects
a historical version when the backend supports versioning.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description:
Optional version selector. When zero (the default), the latest
version is returned. When non-zero, the requested historical
version is returned if the backend retains it; otherwise the RPC
fails with NOT_FOUND.

delete.summary:
DeleteSecret removes a KV secret. Soft delete by default; set
`destroy=true` to hard-delete all versions.

parameters.name: destroy
parameters.description:
When true, permanently remove all versions at the path. Requires
the caller to hold destroy-level ACL on the path.

put.summary:
PutSecret writes a KV secret. Uses optimistic concurrency when
`if_version` is set.

get.summary:
ListSecrets returns paths under a prefix. Prefix is required —
implementations MUST NOT permit listing the entire backend.

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description:
Prefix to list under. Required — callers may not list the entire
backend.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of paths to return. The server may cap the value.

ifVersion.description:
Optional optimistic-concurrency check. When set, the RPC fails
with FAILED_PRECONDITION if the current version at `path` does
not match this value. Use 0 to require that the path does not
yet exist.

SecretsPutSecretBody.description: PutSecretRequest is the request message for PutSecret.

incrementSeconds.description:
Optional requested increment in seconds. The backend may cap the
renewal to its configured maximum; the actual new expiry is
returned in the response.

SecretsRenewLeaseBody.description: RenewLeaseRequest is the request message for RenewLease.

SecretsRevokeLeaseBody.description: RevokeLeaseRequest is the request message for RevokeLease.

leaseId.description:
Identifier of the lease this token is bound to. Used with
RenewLease and RevokeLease.

expiresAt.description: When `token` becomes invalid if not renewed.

renewable.description:
Whether the lease may be renewed. When false, callers that need
continued access must call the Login RPC again to mint a fresh
token.

v1AuthToken.description:
AuthToken is a bearer credential issued by the Secrets backend
against a workload login. Callers present `token` as
`Authorization: Bearer <token>` on subsequent Secrets RPCs.

The token is bound to a lease: it expires at `expires_at` and MUST
be renewed via RenewLease (or re-minted via a fresh Login RPC)
before then. Tokens MAY be revoked early via RevokeLease.

v1DeleteSecretResponse.description:
DeleteSecretResponse is the response message for DeleteSecret. It
is intentionally empty.

v1GetSecretResponse.description: GetSecretResponse is the response message for GetSecret.

paths.description:
Matching paths under `prefix`. Values are returned relative to
the backend root, not relative to `prefix`.

nextPageToken.description:
Opaque pagination token. Empty when there are no further
results.

v1ListSecretsResponse.description: ListSecretsResponse is the response message for ListSecrets.

role.description:
Name of the Secrets-backend role that maps the presenting
ServiceAccount to an ACL policy. Implementations MUST refuse
login when the presented JWT's (namespace, service-account) pair
is not bound to the requested role.

jwt.description:
Projected ServiceAccount token. The implementation passes this
to Kubernetes' TokenReview endpoint for verification; Nightshift
does not specify the projection audience (implementations
configure it to match their Kubernetes cluster's settings).

v1LoginWithKubernetesRequest.description:
LoginWithKubernetesRequest is the request message for
LoginWithKubernetes. The caller presents its projected
ServiceAccount JWT; the implementation verifies it against
Kubernetes' TokenReview API (or equivalent) and returns a scoped
AuthToken.

auth.description: Scoped bearer credential and its lease.

v1LoginWithKubernetesResponse.description:
LoginWithKubernetesResponse is the response message for
LoginWithKubernetes.

secret.description: The written secret, including the newly assigned version.

v1PutSecretResponse.description: PutSecretResponse is the response message for PutSecret.

renewable.description:
Whether the lease remains renewable. When false, callers must
re-authenticate before `expires_at` rather than renewing again.

v1RenewLeaseResponse.description: RenewLeaseResponse is the response message for RenewLease.

v1RevokeLeaseResponse.description:
RevokeLeaseResponse is the response message for RevokeLease. It is
intentionally empty.

path.description:
Opaque path within the Secrets backend. Path semantics and the
rules for which callers may read which paths are defined by the
implementation; see `secrets.md` §3 for the recommended
user-scoped and org-scoped conventions.

data.description: Key/value payload. All values are strings.

version.description:
Monotonic server-assigned version. Each PutSecret produces a new
version; prior versions may be retained by the backend for
rollback. Implementations without versioning SHOULD return a
strictly increasing counter.

createdAt.description: Time the first version at this path was written.

updatedAt.description: Time the most recent version was written.

v1Secret.description:
Secret is a single KV payload at a path. Values are stringly-typed
(`map<string, string>`) to match the KV v2 shape OpenBao and Vault
expose; binary payloads MUST be encoded (for example base64) into
string values by the caller.
